# Transcrição: 9. Care So Much That You Refuse Not To Close

- **Duração:** 03:28

## [00:00 - 03:01]

Okay, rule 18, okay? Care so much that you will refuse not to close. Do you understand what I'm saying? You are going to refuse not to accept them not closing because you care so much. Now, look, this is way beyond selling. Maybe simple to you. Oh, yeah, okay, I'm going to care so much. No, you're going to care so much that you're not going to let them not close. This is beyond selling and more in the line of purpose. And purpose? This is a way higher motivator than money is for everyone. The fact that you're not on a purpose-driven motivation doesn't mean that money's higher than that. Money is the lowest. Purpose is the highest. You want to be purpose-driven if at all possible. But whatever your drive is, whatever your motivation is, you need to care so much about your product, your service, and your client that you won't let them say no to you. An individual can reach incredible heights in their life if they were doing exactly what's on their lines of purpose. That means you have to believe in what you're doing. I don't care if I'm sweeping hallways. I need to believe that that is something. I'm mowing lawns. Look, if the grass didn't get cut in this country, it would cover concrete, highways, and roads. They say that would happen in the first 20 years of every human being left this planet. There'd be all kinds of failures just because nature would take over. Every job is important. That's why you've got to see it as a mission, like your mission. You know, a charity worker. They're on a mission. They work tirelessly. No money. Day and night to make a difference. See, this is missing in your sales. It's missing at the negotiating table and it's missing in the clothes. Purpose, caring. These people don't correctly and completely align selling a product and closing for a profit with any kind of higher purpose. Look at the end of the day, your true purpose will carry you through. What is your purpose? Just to make money? Okay, I want some money, man. I need to make some money. They're going to, I'm going to sell this and they're going to give me this paper. But there's got to be something else. There's got to be something else driving you that makes you care so much about that individual. I'm selling a telephone, an automobile. I'm selling a piece of real estate. You've got to care. You've got to find the care in whatever it is you're selling. Okay? An employee of mine watched me handle a customer over the phone once. I was in my office in Los Angeles. Call comes over and when he heard me handle this man for 20 minutes, he ends by, when I hung up the phone, he says, you know, Grant, the difference between your call and my call is the amount of care that you communicate in helping people. You're not locked, Grant, on selling one product or this product or that product. You're not locked into your financial statement. You're locked into help. You're locked into helping him. What is the best thing to help him right now? Maybe it's somebody else. I have a hard time believing that, but I care. I want to care about the individual.

## [03:01 - 03:28]

It's not how much you want their money or even the clothes. It's how much do you care about people? How much do you want to help another person that will ultimately get you what you want? And you want to help them and believe, have a deep belief about that because you do believe in your product, your service, your company, and you believe in yourself and your ability to serve and take care of people. Care more for them. And I promise you, you will close more deals.
